name: Tidal volume per body weight (Vt/kg)
description: Tidal volume normalised to body weight, in mL/kg (lung-protective ventilation target). Absolute mL form is separate.

concept.conceptName: Tidal volume.spontaneous+mechanical/Body weight [Volume/mass] corrected for compressible gas volume --on ventilator

en.name: Tidal volume per body weight (Vt/kg)
en.shortDescription: Tidal volume normalised to body weight, in mL/kg (lung-protective ventilation target). Absolute mL form is separate.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Tidal volume per body weight is the tidal volume normalised to the patient's body weight (usually predicted/ideal body weight), the central target of lung-protective ventilation (e.g. 6 mL/kg). In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, reported in millilitres per kilogram (mL/kg). It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients.

## Included Concepts

The set lists the LOINC tidal-volume-per-body-weight concepts (mL/kg).

- *3016864 / 20120-2 — Tidal volume.spontaneous+mechanical/Body weight [Volume/mass] corrected for compressible gas volume --on ventilator (LOINC)*
- *3017054 / 20115-2 — Tidal volume.spontaneous/Body weight [Volume/mass] --on ventilator (LOINC)*
- *3017878 / 20117-8 — Tidal volume.spontaneous+mechanical/Body weight [Volume/mass] --on ventilator (LOINC)*

**Note on units**: reported in **mL/kg** (`9571 / mL/kg — milliliter per kilogram`, UCUM), the recommended unit.

## Mapping Notes

- Map a weight-indexed tidal volume (mL/kg) to one of these concepts.
- **Not inter-convertible with absolute tidal volume**: the absolute tidal volume (mL) is a separate concept set; converting between mL and mL/kg requires the patient's (predicted) body weight, so the two are kept as separate, unit-homogeneous sets.
- LOINC also expresses these per pound (mL/lb); convert to mL/kg before mapping if needed.

fr.name: Volume courant rapporté au poids (Vt/kg)
fr.shortDescription: Volume courant rapporté au poids, en mL/kg (cible de ventilation protectrice). La forme absolue en mL est un set distinct.
